methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Astrolight is a seed-stage Space & Aerospace company founded by Laurynas Mačiulis that has raised €2.8M to date. The valuation landscape for early-stage space companies is sparse; the sector contains 23 same-stage peers overall, but the comparable universe for narrow peer-matching (same stage, adjacent stage, similar valuation tier) yields fewer than three datapoints, making traditional peer-median analysis inapplicable. Public Space & Aerospace comprises 6 traded companies (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) with a median price-to-sales multiple of 64.2x, and the private peer set includes established names such as SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace—though these represent a materially different stage and scale.

Standard triangulation methods face binding constraints. Peer-median multiples cannot be reliably extracted due to insufficient narrow-stage comparables. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple is structurally blocked because Astrolight, as a private pre-revenue or early-revenue company, does not disclose revenue figures required for multiple application. The 6-ticker public sector median of 64.2x is too broad to apply directly to a seed-stage venture.

Without disclosed financial metrics (revenue, EBITDA, or growth rates) and given the acute sparsity of seed-stage Space & Aerospace comps, a defensible valuation range cannot be triangulated from the data available. Key uncertainties include the company's revenue traction, capital efficiency relative to sector norms, and technology differentiation versus the identified peer set. Additional data on Astrolight's recurring revenue or unit economics would be necessary to anchor a credible valuation.
